channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: AirFox — 46 replies — 61.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
AirFox

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-07-17 12:06 UTC

OP

Any qrd on what’s going on with this thing? What exactly are these guys hoping to accomplish? Crypto is cooked and they keep trying to change this project in ways that don’t seem to matter. The TG link also doesn’t work anymore.

Early replies (first 3)

62481067
>>62480640
>2 hours later
>0 replies
jfc it’s worse than I thought. These threads used to reach bump limits after a couple hours.

62481088
>>62480640
it's a dead shitcoin, the devs are just waiting for the few final bagholders to give up so they can wrap it up and enjoy the money they made selling this shit

62481280
>>62480640
its just a rebrand and a relaunch with shifty eyes about robinhood instead of base.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482861
this coin is driving me insane

62482868
>>62482861
Why don't you play some FUD Free Fox in the aviator arcade if you need to blow off some steam?

62482872
>>62481704
>2023 and early 2024 were comfy

not really (sure the chart went up), they hedged way too much with Base and they ghosted the fuck out of them. the shitposting was fun

>>62482868
ill play that penguin game too

>>62481415
>$300k mcap
that's nothing, if it hits 5-10m without any massive selloff to 1m ill be interested

62483375
>>62482872
I acknowledge that the team messed up along the way but it was still a comfy hold early on. The tg was chill, the team was active, the AMAs were something to look forward to, and overall everyone had hope. Then all the “early frens and supporters” and paw drama started happening, the kerasu saga, the obvious signs they gave up on the arcade in favor of the bridge that no one cared for, the on chain summer BS, ambiguous messages trying to lead us on, etc. It just continued going down hill from there.

62483861
kek the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours. I remember them lasting for at least a few days, even a couple months before they dumped. This isn’t looking so good

62484514
kek jimmy won.
matthew red won.
cryptoempress won.
rowdy randy won.
maxpain won.
sumdog won.
shadowlord won.
aviator lost. airfox? LMAO you have no liquidity. VC abandoned stratus. Remember the 1 cent per AVI? LOL. magic is gone btw? is mitch gone? is stixil the only one that remains? holders quit their jobs at tim hortons for this coin and THIS is how you repay them?

62484570
don't listen to the haters stixil. If you keep trying you will eventually succeed and show everyone was wrong. I do suggest putting back the liquidity, its a huge red flag taking the liq out due to lack of funds as it signals users you are out of money which could lead to team token sales to fund the project coming from the communities pockets.

62484686
>>62484514
Magic hasn’t been in the tg for probably a year now? It’s said he hangs out in discord but the discord seemed dead last time I checked. He was also supposedly heavily involved with V2s development. I don’t know who is left anymore because I can’t even join the tg.

>>62484570
>don't listen to the haters stixil. If you keep trying you will eventually succeed and show everyone was wrong
Falko? Anyways, I ask in good faith. Besides the obvious trolling, what has been said so far that is wrong? Stixil clearly lacks any direction for where he wants to take this project. He has jumped from one idea to another any time something doesn’t work out. When V2 launched and got no traction, he immediately announced that they will take over stratus and for us to ‘stay tuned’. When that eventually doesn’t work out, he will have already came up with another new thing to keep people hanging on. It’s been like that for the last 3 years and it will continue being that way for as long as avi is around

62484716
>>62481088
They rebranded, increased token supply for themselves and airdropped everyone the new tokens.

62485941
>>62483861
>the fake pump did t even hold for 24 hours
I hope no one thought that mini pump was organic. It is just one of many that happened in the last couple of years and it’s probably a tg member trying to farm liquidity. It’s sad that this is what the volume is based on. Some bot / poorfag trying to farm $10-$50 from some other poorfags who got baited into buying a green chart lol

62486102
where is the fucking liquidity i cant sell what the fuck i need my money now. airfox? really? where is my cent per AVI that was promised to me

62486284
>>62486102
There is $11k liquidity. Thats more than enough

62486513
>$300k mcap
Yea I’m thinking it’s over

62486525
>>62486513
$80m whale is starting to sell. A former six figure bag is now worth $4k. Ouch

62487100
>>62484514
The Schizo also won and the red baron roped.
Flawless Victory

>>62481067
>>62481088
Anon, crypto is dead just as this board.
can't you fucking tell this already after two years?
The days of making 5 figs or even 3 figs on ETH shitcoins is over.
retail sadly migrated to solana and crime is legal now
those days of quick money on ETH and biz shitcoins are not coming back

62487573
>>62486525
a few bags were worth 7 figs at the height (yeah yeah, muh liquidity, I know) silly fuckers held all this time, that's gotta hurt, a few got out with 6 figs but fucked the chart

62487601
>>62486284
whoa, wtf? hadn't even spotted that, 9k now.....pffffffhahahahah

62487664
>>62487100
Solana is part of why crypto got fucked imo. Outside of the scammers and a few degens, I can’t imagine too many people making money there. But yea I don’t see any light at the end of the tunnel for crypto as a whole

62489045
Damn we hit $200k. Which one of you fucks is selling?

62489432
>>62481067
If you haven't noticed, anon, jannies and jeets wrung every drop of juice out of this board for years. I still come back on occasion and reply out of habit, just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread. /biz/ is a fucking ghost town

62489758
Wait a minute tho, if he does actually launch this token launcher etc for robin hood, it could be one of the early launches and go to the moon

62489887
>>62489432
>just to find the majority of my posts are the end of a thread
lol I thought it was just me. I’ll go into what seems like an active thread and as soon as I post a reply, that shit dies. It’s weird

>>62489758
>robinhood
I’m very skeptical about sentiment going back to what it was. I honestly don’t think crypto will ever be a space where we see 100x’s across the board. Even 2024s run was shit and I don’t think we will see that either. I see robinhood as another Base type of thing. Granted, Base did pretty good in the beginning but it ultimately turned out to be a chain for insider trading. That’s why crypto is in the state that it’s in. Nothing but a bunch of jews

62489964
>>62489045
>down 27% off less than 4k volume
yeah this shit is cooked.

62490304
>>62489887
>I see robinhood as another Base type of thing
I don’t even see it as that. Remember how Base was supposed to onboard 1b people? Remember how bad the on chain summer was? Coinbase was, and still is, one of the most well known names in the space and they couldn’t even pull it off. People stopped caring. I hope I’m wrong but all the things are leading to RH being another nothing burger

62490407
>>62487664
Yeah Scamlana fucked the shitcoin market and stole too much liquidity. And before that, a new useless ghost chain copy pasted version of ETH being created every week.
it's ogre for ETH and the shitcoin market overall
>T. Roundtripped a $110k Apu bag and held like a good baggie

62490814
>>62490407
>roundtripped
That’s the thing that worries me if we ever did have another bull run. The dumps were just as quick as the pumps and a lot of people were caught off guard. Too many baggies were created and many are still holding on, including whales. I see that across a lot of the stuff I’m in. So if we ever did have a run up I feel like the sell pressure would be worse than before because everyone that’s still holding will want to exit with whatever profits they can get. All the shitcoins with little liquidity are going to get nuked because of how pvp it will get. I’m a baggie who round tripped a mid-upper 6 figure bag across a bunch of projects and I can tell you right now that my standards of what I consider to be “making it” is far less than before. idgaf about making 7 figures anymore. Even getting 1/3 of my ATH is enough for me to exit and I’m sure a lot of people feel this way

62490948
>>62489045
>Which one of you fucks is selling?
lmao, you must be new here

62491616
I just noticed that 60m wallet that’s selling is an early wallet that has ties to paw / shib related stuff. I’m getting ‘frens and supporters’ vibes with this one. It’s likely the team knows who owns that wallet. It’s never going to end…..

62493068
and with that…avi’s story has come to a close. happy third birthday aviator. the final day . i am done. goodbye. 3 years too long. it’s not cozy anymore its just sad. i just cant do this anymore im sorry

item.title: If you could sell 1 IQ for $5000 how many IQ points would you sell? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you could sell 1 IQ for $5000 how many IQ points would you sell?

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-07-17 12:50 UTC

OP

If you could sell 1 IQ for $5000 how many IQ points would you sell?

Early replies (first 3)

62491920
>>62491899
40

62491924
10

62491925
Gee if only it were possible to figure out how much money people make on avg based on their iq so you could come up with a realistic figure instead of 5 fucking k

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62491939
>>62491899
900

62491957
>>62491899
i'd probably be happier as a 120 iq midwit so maybe

62491958
>>62491899
>implying I have IQ points

62491973
>>62491957
The way you write I doubt you even reach an IQ of 60

62492013
>>62491957
same
high intelligence sucks ass and doesn't pay

62492124
>>62491899
$5000 is how much a 100 IQ person (in the united states) makes a month. You need to pump those numbers up.

62492142
>>62491899
selling your IQ OP? I'd be in debt by about 5000 usd

62492143
>>62491899
I'd be buying at those prices.

62493147
>>62491973
>11 words is "so much text"
oof

62493183
>>62491899
5k? That's a joke. For 500k I would sell a couple if I'm in my 20s. Nowadays I'd sell a couple for 1M each.
If you're willing to sell them for 5k, sell 20 to me, I'll gladly pay tjis rate.

62493207
>>62492143
/bread

item.title: ETH/BTC — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ETH/BTC

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-07-17 12:49 UTC

OP

Why is it trying to break out again? Why now?

Early replies (first 3)

62492230
>>62492200
>trying
You mean failing, right? It is failing to break out again, because it is a PoS.

62492253
>>62492200
I am holding only eth, it wont pump. ITS NOT ALLOWED TO PUMP, I AM BOGGED, NO WAY IT LL BREAK OUT, I BOUGHT SO MUCH UNDER 2000$. RELEASE ZE BINANCE "HACK" REALEASE ZE WORLD WAR 3!
HEAT DEAT OF THE UNIVERSE IMMINENT! GFC NOWWW!

62492325
>>62492253
I even have a gif for this.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62492336
>>62492200
that unironically looks like a meme line that's well supported by the chart for once (except that it should start in 2022). i noticed other ETH ratios like SOL/ETH or BNB/ETH are also crashing which is rather unusual.

62492355
>>62492200
crapto is dead unc

62492360
>>62492355
I slowly sold my crypto during 2024-2025. Fully out now

62492438
>>62492336
Its captured.

62492681
>>62492336
>i noticed other ETH ratios like SOL/ETH or BNB/ETH are also crashing which is rather unusual.
Smaller coins falling against ETH in a bear market is very much to be expected though.

62492999
>>62492200
because this is when it is least expected. but you already know that op. you're just being a fucking tool ass fag.

62493042
>>62492681
yeah but this is a more recent phenomenon. something happened in the beginning of July that made ETH rise much more than these other big altcoins, sometimes even with green days on ETH/USD where the others went down against USD. especially BNB and TRON usually tend to rise against ETH in a bear market.

62493198
>>62492200
Lost 0.029, COOL!

OP

/pol/ knows more about what is happening on the global markets than /biz/.
This board just spamming threads with pepes, shitcoins(i.e. LINK, XRP) and about hating normies.

>>>/pol/539102367

Early replies (first 3)

62492931
>>62492926
global mass sell off by pedo elites. they've been selling off on the dark market for a while now. those effects are just now reaching retail. rip. tidy up boyos.

62492936
>>62492926
Neither board can tell their own head from their arse. If you are somewhat educated in finance you mostly come here to laugh at the financially uninformed, and as reassurance that things could be much worse.

62492959
>>62492926
>AHH china is down 5%!!
Tell me right now what you're actually going to do as a result of this information.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62492997
>>62492926
My Bank of China shares are doing well.

62493115
Why is it that /pol/ and /biz/ are my 2 favorite boards?

62493173
>>62492931
> dark market
just finished fucking your mom; your breakfast is getting cold, go upstairs little johnny

62493180
>>62492926
It's just a low volume dump due to summer time vacation + world cup. Wait until July 26 and everything will be green again. Except oil.

item.title: >Aave v4 launches on Avalanche >They start an incentive system >Look inside >Single-digit APR Look what they took from u… — 4 replies — 6.1/hr

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-17 12:12 UTC

OP

>Aave v4 launches on Avalanche
>They start an incentive system
>Look inside
>Single-digit APR
Look what they took from us! A real incentivized launch used to be 3000% APR

Early replies (first 3)

62490287
>>62490276
You're brown for even trying to discuss avax

62490325
>>62490287
Ofc it's a shitty chain
But 3% incentives
That's ridiculous for the amount of risks taken

62490728
>>62490325
it's not really, aave is still relatively trustworthy despite their governance going full retarded this year

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 100 replies — 135.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-07-17 12:07 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62485172
>>62481986
This article is applicable not only to Paris, but Europe as a whole.

62485475
>>62485172
Are americans really proud that they commit crimes like immigrants? At least the problem here is only the fresh arrivals you have rednecks shooting each other.

62485570
>>62481958
One good thing about the UK is how flexible ISAs are, and how, if you have a biz, you can pay fairly low amount of tax. If you're PAYE, the UK is rough.

62485700
>>62485475
>if I'm just egregiously disingenuous and dishonest, surely that will change somebody's mind!
Go glow somewhere else

62485922
>>62481986
You brits are so utterly cattle brained you're not even worth saving anymore. Yeah dude you're actually a global superpower since you're India 2.0 at this point and as everyone knows India superpower 2030 saar trust. Go fuck yourself. I actually hope your country melts into an acultured amalgam before it implodes and disappears from the face of this world.

62485926
>>62482009

How dare you post racist comment ! Big Brother's enforcers are on their way.

62486257
>>62482110
yeah think this is what pisses people off the most, like the pure SCALE.. if we roll back the clock no one would mind maybe even a number like 10,000 (thats still ALOT of people) but manageable.. whereas obviously having 500k a year is stupidly outrageous thats a whole city every year

and lefties like "omg thats racist u have to equally accommodate for the 7 billion other people in the world" no mr leftie lets be honest if this was about an amazon tribe or even GAZA population you would NOT agree letting 500,000 white people into their population for mur rights

62486263
>>62486257
they would call that COLONIALISM

62486459
Part of the reason is that you voted for brexit without really knowing what that entailed and then you reaped the consequences of more bureaucracy and red tape. Makes you look kind of foolish

62487118
>>62486459
you have no idea what youre talking about

62487400
>>62485922
The Royal Navy is the third strongest navy on the planet. The UK is a superpower we just don't play team america and bomb the middle east for sport.

62487495
>>62481958
We wuz kangs!
We wuz empyres n shieeet!
We brown muzlem now

62487523
>>62481958
Brexit fucked everything up

62487539
>>62487523
You voted for this

62490030
>>62487539
We voted for sovereignty from unelected european bureaucrats. We voted to take Britain back and undo the mistakes we made under the EU.

>>62487523
Brexit is the best thing to happen to this country in decades. There are teething pains because europe refuses to accept us leaving and make deals with us but we are free to put the Great back into Britain.

62490071
>>62481958
Muhammad has been the most popular baby name for boys for 3 years in a row

62490585
A lot of it is shills/terminally online retards. The entire county I live is is 97% white (last census so probs a bit lower) and we've just bought a nice 400k house on a combined salary of 90k. I can and do walk/cycle around in full safety at any time of day. Maybe some smack head might look at me wrong but that's the worst of it. I would rather live here than America and most of Europe every day of the week

62490813
>>62490585
I'd ask where that is but I wouldn't want to blow it for you
Assuming its not a larp, godspeed anon

62490838
Sometimes I feel like responding to Europoors. Like I live in a capital city just minutes on foot to restaurants, shops, grocery stores. I pay less than 1k USD for my apartment. I can walk to work. I feel 100 percent safe, and could carry a firearm or knife if I so pleased but I am not a pussy. I often think about replying to Europoors and then I remember this meme.

62490850
>>62490585
Great post. A brit brangs about being able to walk/cycle outside and not getting stabbed.

62492082
>>62490850
bitter

62492388
>>62492082
Bitter about what?

62492412
>>62487400
>pictured: the entire UK fleet right before being crippled by their annual necessary repairs

62492424
>>62481973
>here
You need to get out before youre arrested for posting that

62492526
>>62492388
In context of the thread I was hardly bragging. I dare you to post where you live so we can have a go too

62492845
>>62492526
I live in a very expensive area created by corrupt political nepo insiders for corrupt political nepo insiders. Everybody has 2-3 newest Audi or mercedes SUVs and several millions in the bank. All the houses are 3,000+ square feet and have gardens. But not me, I'm an outsider here, and everybody knows that because they all know each other and that I'm not part of their club. All the neighbors are professional dogshit cunt parasites. So yeah you live in a better place.

62492892
>>62490838
>and could carry a firearm or knife

Love it how you're so compelled to mention this but are too afraid to use them for real because you'll be locked up.

Feel free to use it in a school or mall though. Seems like the average American pastime.

62492960
>>62492845
why would you move there? Sounds stupid

62493072
>>62481958
>Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs.
You can neither walk nor talk safely.
It's a beautiful country, it WAS a nice culture otherwise.
Now, if you have a daughter, or are a fuckable woman, you will get raped if not tortured by some gang, if you complain you will be arrested for racism, if you protest you will get beaten by the same worthless bobbies who refused to help, and it's all part of the meanest large scale society destuction, ethnic replacement, power grab and money grab back to feudalism ever in European history.
The economy is not better.

62493208
>>62492960
Because I'm a poorfag retard, it's not my place and I can live here for free.

item.title: both Swift and DTCC are going live right now — 162 replies — 95.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
both Swift and DTCC are going live right now

Open thread • Replies: 162 • Updated: 2026-07-17 11:10 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62466455
Price?

62466457
>>62466455
Suppressed,

62466461
And literal top-buying turned full time fudders are finally capitulating.

Imagine subjecting yourself to all the pain and never seeing it through the end. I gave myself 6 months after SWIFT and DTCC are live to see if the token shoots up, and then decide wether to sell half my stack of just staking rewards.

But to sell RIGHT BEFORE the literal ANNOUNCED DATES of the two biggest financial behemoths' launches? That's crazy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62486012
>>62486001
oh and this one

62486018
this is happening btw
DTCC live in production, with Chainlink.

62486482
>>62486018
They are pumping bitcoin to suppress chainlink.

62486630
so now DTCC will just gradually pump link's price forever right sinkies? ya'll gonna make it?
you'd think linkies would be more enthused. maybe deep down they know this means nothing

62487460
>>62486630
Cry along now, xerpxerp

62487501
>>62466645
That’s called projection. You’re clearly a xrp retard. Lying retards. Brad clearly stated to CLG that youre all cash cows

62487520
>>62467154
You need to look into how much failed trx cost banks each year. That alone is a huge incentive. Then there’s also a huge suit of frictionless financial products.
Kek fuddies

62487535
>>62487501
>Brad clearly stated to CLG that youre all cash cows
that was the fat slimy kike David, not Brad, but yeah, he literally told people
>well XRP dropping like a stone is GOOD ackshually because... you can buy more at a cheaper price!!
>XRP is not a sercurity. Ripple's XRP is Ripple's to use to advance their business interests. If you'd prefer exposure to all of Ripple's business, you can buy stock in Ripple.

And the crippletard cucks will take it in the ass like the good, raped slaves they are LMAO

62487575
>>62487501
Isn't it crazy to think that all the Link fud these past 6-7 years came from xerpies?

Even Adem is just out in the open with it now; this whole thread is him defending XRP from getting called out over the xerpie DTCC delusion: >>62484143

62487582
>>62487535
>>62487575
sirs XRP is bad token, please invest in Chainlink if you want the village well fed.

Praise krishna

62487621
>>62487582
>xerpie #36 enters the Chainlink-Swift-DTCC chat

You guys have been living in link threads this past half decade.

62488189
>>62486482
>They are pumping bitcoin to suppress chainlink.

"pumping bitcoin"?
Pic is bitcoin right from the DTCC press release that mentioned Chainlink

62488200
>>62488189
fuck you bloody the bitcoin whales are suppressing us bitch

62488211
>>62488200
well it's definitely not pumping is it?

You suppression deniers get so triggered that you're hallucinating your deboonkings

62488218
>>62488211
one day chainlink will pump to one million and we will all be rich. I believe!

62488229
>>62488211
fuck you bastard bich

62488315
>>62472871
>(PA version 2 is said to automate this)
payment abstraction is doing this. Its just changing some shit around from uniswap to cow swap and adding a dutch auction. it does nothing to adress the buying tokens problem

62488326
>>62488315
>buying tokens on the open market does not address the buying tokens problem

?

62488334
>>62488326
sir why is link at 8 dollars my village is hungry. Big partnership dtcc will pump it right?

62488492
>>62488326
they are outpacing the buys with dumps. you still are dancing around that you absolute imbecile

62488527
>>62488492
All cryptos do that. They all have inflation that far outstrips usage fees.

62488547
>>62488527
how much are you getting paid to post on here? there is no way you are just here saying the same wrong shit over and over again and believe it

62488569
>>62488547
>how much are you getting paid to post on here?

says the faggot blaming Chainlink specifically for the most standard crypto behaviors

62488802
>>62488569
please the village is hungry

62489225
>>62466449
That's cool and all... unfortunately it has zero impact on the token price, so who the fuck cares that Sergey and his minions will rake in another billion in the future.

62492292
>>62486482
>they are pumping bitcoin

62492353
>>62466449
What exactly "went live" here? Was it used for anything? Is there any suggestion that this most recent round of techno-jibbajabba has seen any application?

62492372
>>62492353
https://www.dtcc.com/news/2026/july/15/dtcc-turns-tokenization-into-reality

"successfully converted assets held at The Depository Trust Company (DTC) into tokens that were then used in real production trades"

"The event featured several transactions made across various asset classes in a DTC production environment, including collateral pledge, security lending, U.S. Treasury/repo delivery-versus-payment (DVP) trade, equity DVP trade, equity delivery-versus-delivery (DVD) trade, equity token transfer and central counterparty (CCP) margin workflows."

62492759
>>62492372
one-time pilot, got it
>inb4 now wait for October bro

62492898
>>62492759
someone's salty his coin wasn't involved.

Open thread • Replies: 53 • Updated: 2026-07-17 11:59 UTC

OP

Buy SPCX. $135 is the absolute floor. It will be at $260 within 12 months. You will hate yourself if you don't get in now.

Early replies (first 3)

62483359
>>62483350
4%

62483366
>>62483359
?

62483376
>>62483366

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62486741
it is likely to go lower once the early investors can sell their stock

62487453
>>62484142
that would be a conflict of interest of course
>>62484127
basically guaranteed
>>62484447
rural areas

62487473
>>62483350
Hahaha you boughted the IPO?
Sorry man, not buying your bag until it's 40~60.

62487479
Pre IPO I thought ultimately the reason why the float was low was so they could clandestinely support a level until the lockups are done.
Haven't changed my mind yet but that number might not be 135.

62488213
>>62483350
not buying your shitty stock

62489975
>>62486692

62490634
>>62489975
Is this an illustration of how Starlink works?

62490969
if you assume they eventually take over most of the Internet market, which they probably will, then they're going to 100x over that time. there will always be a small market for wired Internet for latency reasons, etc, but
starlink will handle 95%+ of internet traffic eventually.

62490980
>>62490969
lol

62490999
wasnt this shit like $220 just a few weeks ago? kek

62491010
>>62483350
Last time I came here you faggots were calling everyone who didn't buy poorfags for not jumping in at $180. Now you're telling me 135 is THE ABSOLUTE floor?

I won't be buying your bags, ranjeet.

62491015
>>62486545
You can send rockets with big surface area to knock the debris away from orbit and this way SPCX makes money on the launches and remediation launches too

62491067
>>62490999
>>62491010
$120 is the absolute floor. For real this time. 100%. Please buy.
>>62490969
why do people need satellites when fiber is cheaper and faster? only advantage is availability, but fiber matches that for most.

62491121
>>62483366
You deserve to lose everything.

62491155
>>62486686
You're going to pump my mining bags several times over before anyone starts mining asteroids. If no one is willing to invest in mines with proven reserves and toptier cash flow on earth why the fuck would anyone want to invest on an unproven method? If they aren't investing in spcx for mineral extraction then what exactly are baggies "investing" in? Read the prospectus on the company you are shilling, they want to be an AI company that used to build rockets. Elon is a poison pill for the company and cannot be fired due to structure. He just wants more money for datacenters despite not investing in the prerequisite minerals for any of his enterprises. I'm sure china will be happy to provide these minerals to him and all the other companies openly boasting about being in an arms race against china.

62491183
>>62483350
ok, everyone who is not OP
how low will it go?
will it ever rebound?
a good price to pick up shares?

62491228
>>62491183
Wait for what the orange jew says in 1 minute. If he's shilling some retarded war or increasing government spending in a manner to enrich his friends come back in six months. If he miraculously announces a ceasefire, come back in 6 days when he's threatening to glass them again. If the ceasefire somehow holds wait for the lockup period to end, timelines are available, and after majority is sold then observe and wait for floor to develop. If the economy is not functioning due to WW3 and an energy crisis followed by a recession buy a company that makes money instead. How do you fairly value a company that's prospectus is entirely memes, 18 pages of rocket photos and one net positive business drowned by 5 cash burning ones? You can't, no one can. Wait until its not dominating the news cycle and then buy it. I bought coinbases IPO, and had to wait quite some time to make money. Even if a company or sector is good, IPOs are best left to idiots. Buy things that are hated but for bad reasons, look at gold or uuuu as an example rather than financial advice. Everything bounces back eventually unless it doesn't. SPCX is hated for good reason and its barely even begun yet. Your goal shoul be to baghold as little as possible and we are entering a period where it may take 5+ years if you buy the wrong thing.

62491258
>>62491228
>Wait for what the orange jew says in 1 minute. If he's shilling some retarded war or increasing government spending in a manner to enrich his friends come back in six months. If he miraculously announces a ceasefire, come back in 6 days when he's threatening to glass them again. If the ceasefire somehow holds wait for the lockup period to end, timelines are available, and after majority is sold then observe and wait for floor to develop. If the economy is not functioning due to WW3 and an energy crisis followed by a recession buy a company that makes money instead. How do you fairly value a company that's prospectus is entirely memes, 18 pages of rocket photos and one net positive business drowned by 5 cash burning ones? You can't, no one can. Wait until its not dominating the news cycle and then buy it. I bought coinbases IPO, and had to wait quite some time to make money. Even if a company or sector is good, IPOs are best left to idiots. Buy things that are hated but for bad reasons, look at gold or uuuu as an example rather than financial advice. Everything bounces back eventually unless it doesn't. SPCX is hated for good reason and its barely even begun yet. Your goal shoul be to baghold as little as possible and we are entering a period where it may take 5+ years if you buy the wrong thing.
Are you ok? This post sounds like you need to take some sort of medication. Orange man does not in fact control everyone in the whole world. surely, you can make some analysis of this company, its fundamental, the human psychology around it, or any other of a number of variables without trying to screed about spraytan man. do you have a discord? because you post seems to heavily imply you do.

62491327
>>62491258
>analysis
I did, but its worth noting what oil prices did in 2008 and what followed. If you don't understand that price action can be affected by 2 or more factors simultaneouly then I suggest you ignore everything I said and consider $120 the new floor.

62491346
Desperate

62491495
>>62491183
120 is the floor but it will be $200 by EOY

62491881
>>62491495
Ok. Why

62491884
>>62483350
>Buy SPCX
SpaceKEKS

62491889
>>62483350
i rarely post here but ill save some newfags some money...


everyone should wait about 6 months, then decide.


way too early now. lots of down comin

62492490
>>62491495

62492529
>>62483350
It would do well if it didn't add xAI to the pile which is a money black hole just like every other AI

62492905
>>62483350
If it drops below 100 I'll consider it.

62492909
>>62491495
Floor bros....the floor keeps lowering

62493017
>how did you arrive at that price target

62493049
>>62492529
That's why it went public. To sell the AI top, and get CAPEX moneis. He needed to piggyback xAI on two hyper successful, wide moat businesses.

item.title: Everest MiCA CASP — 99 replies — 55.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Everest MiCA CASP

Open thread • Replies: 99 • Updated: 2026-07-17 11:04 UTC

OP

I want to personally thank every Everest ID hater in here, talking to you Link baggies. They secured MiCA CASP last week.

A single MFSA license is valid across 27 EU states, reaching ~450M people. Of the ~283 CASPs on the ESMA register, nearly all are exchanges or brokers. Everest is one of the rare licensed infrastructure providers: identity and payment rails, not another venue.

Enjoy it furries

Early replies (first 3)

62461998
>>62461988
I thought it would have mooned harder from the news. What gives

62462006
It is because we are meant to suffer as token holders just like the Linkies

62463131
>Unlisted from coingecko
Lmao!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62484212
BUMP! That was a close one! Cmon eversisters, we have to do better at keeping this thread alive

62484271
The price keeps rising holy shit

62484289
Don’t fall for this transphobic scam. Ever since my MtF surgery Bob refuses to update my biometrics and now I have no access to tokens.

62484582
>>62484271
He’ll yea, brother! We went from $1.22m to $1.28m! Nocoiners on suicide watch! Watch them cope, seethe, and dial the number 8! Now check my freaking digits!

62485043
>>62484582
Check now :) just getting started too

62485430
>>62485043
>$1.2m to $1.4m
>smugface.jpeg
Heck freaking yea. We are sooo totally gonna make it! I can hear the haters dial8’ing as we speak ;^)

62485733
Haters better get their rope ready

62485738
May the 4 Chan mods please pin this super important thread?

62485915
>>62485733
That’s right, BJ Bob. I just bought 150 shares of CopeRope™ because the HATERS are so totally going to rope themselves haha

62486225
I’ve tried to rope the last 5 years to save myself the pain from holding this dog shit token but here we are today kek

62486253
>>62486225
Hey eversister, you forgot to change your ID. Please delete before others see this

62486286
Holy retard yes I’m the original poster. I’m not a feg like some of you 4channers with 10 alts to boost your little egos, grow up tardos. Bob will shit in your faces and my years of mental disease will magically disappear

62487330
Just commenting during my 3 am middle of night parasite dump, ID still exists

62487641
>>62487330
Thanks for saving the thread. I was asleep and didn’t wake up in time to bump it lol

62487651
fake buys going on rn

62487724
Whats the sui / make it stack?

62487799
The forEverest schizophrenics lighting their USD and Euro on fire the last 5 years would say 1m ID sui

62487913
10m ID, we need a few whales, I’m talking absolute mammoth booty sitting on my face until I die from asphyxiation type of whale

62487917
>>62487799
>>62487913
??
I am not buying 12k or 120k of this 1.4mcap shitcoin

62487944
Go look at marketcap of other tokens with MiCA CASP that Binance could not get lol

62488146
>>62487917
100k sui 400k make it

62488344
We need one of the holders to go through a humiliation ritual, I think that’s when it will start running and never return to earth again

62489235
>>62470842
good. fuck that guy
can't believe he turned his show into an Everest shill

62489843
Bob said he is a mix of Kobe and Tiger Woods the other week. I don’t know much about sports but I think those are very good players who won championships!

62490258
>>62489843
Nigger worshipping is bearish retard

62490777
He also said Tom Brady but those were only 3. I guess that evens it out

62490860
I am indian and i bought, sounds buliesh

62491099
Bob gave Lionel Messi a bath when Messi was a baby. Think about that for a second.

62492644
Good Morning Sherpas! Happy Friday as I take my morning parasite dump

62492894
>>62492644

item.title: so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L… — 98 replies — 26.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink L…

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-07-17 09:12 UTC

OP

so when DTCC and Swift actually start using chainlink for their every day operations, wouldn't that mean the chainlink LLC would be making boatloads of cash from the fees? And wouldn't these fees be used to buy chainlink on the open market, thus impacting the price positively?

Early replies (first 3)

62476682
>>62476675
He is not necessary.

62476690
>>62476675
your logic is absolutely correct. This will result in BTC dumping like its life depends on it.

62476730
>>62476675
Inb4 tranny anime poster

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62481670
>>62480232
>How's the Swift takeover going, cripplet?
>Where are the "over 100 banks" bro?
>Where are the "largest Swift-enabled financial institutions"?
>It's been 8 years, where is everything?

I dont know what you're even talking about.
Im asking the same questions and CLL have not achieved anything.
What is there left for them to do that will increase the token price

62481678
>>62481602
Watching people fight over XRP and LINK is like watching people fight over whether Marvel or DC is better. they're both for faggots and you'd have to be literally retarded to like either

62481749
>>62481670
You're very clearly the verbose cripple shill.
You've been obsessed with CLG ever since he started shitting on Xerp five-six years ago.

62481761
>>62481678
it's not like that at all
marvel and DC are both successful franchises
xrp is just a scam

62482151
>>62481749
You're talking on fucking riddles you retard. I don't even know who xerp is.
I've literally just debunked all of your claims and now you're making shit up

62482524
>>62479364
>XRP
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SHAKALA. I am new to 4chan but have been loving this meme lately! I read on reddit yesterday that this would happen and I boughted (but unfortunately the price basically immediately soon after). I was still so pumped I went to Costco and got a slice of pizza and sat facing those walking out looking at everyone walking by knowing and rye-ly smiling knowing none of them understood what is happening. I felt like Brownstone Capital with Ben Rickerts in the Big Short. I had to stop myself from celebrating because I remembered they were missing out and I wanted to feel the same type of compassion they were reminded to feel by Ben in that moment so I finished my pizza solemnly leaving a bite out of clenched stomach respect for them not being able to finish my meal, grabbed my bag, put my hood up, and walked out nodding to the door inspector knowing looking at them knowingly and humanly as I walked out with them smiling not knowing beneath my shades how I was really seeing them. It was an amazing experience and it felt like everyone was watching but I know they weren't because the thing that made it special was that they don't understand or see. I will come back and tip the kitchen staff handsomely (especially the girl who I like but have not had the courage to ask out....yet) when we are making bookoo bucks and I can afford to own the whole store and promote her. and then smirk as I tell her I will sell the store, non-chalantly, so that I can ask her so she would feel comfortable if I ask her out on a date after I made her general manager of the store. I can't wait!

62483155
>>62482524
Anyone reading this linkie written post, for your own entertainment also see:
https://warosu.org/biz/thread/62479839#p62482964

62483160
>>62481678
Not everything is a sunken dichotomy and being non-committal doesn't make you transcendent, it just makes you a more narcissistic NPC. link is trash. XRP is The Standard.
>>62481749
>Everyone who disagrees with me is the same person
He is not me, I am me.

62483496
I think it depends on how the fee model actually works. Institutional adoption doesn't automatically mean constant buy pressure unless part of the revenue is consistently tied to acquiring LINK from the open market. I'd wait to see how the economics play out instead of assuming usage alone guarantees price appreciation. I'm also interested in how Candora Agent will help analyze market events and sentiment once it launches.

62483559
>>62482151
>I've literally just debunked all of your claims
kek yeah, you debunked the basic utility of the Link token by saying the OG whitepaper doesn't matter.
You're a fucking loon.

And you've been crying about CLG and his wallets for many years while also shilling XRP with the same ID.
We all know it's you.

62484352
>>62483559
I've not mentioned XRP once
This is why LINKies are doomed to be poor and why Sergey has a net worth of 700 million. The lack of basic comprehension and not being able to see the truth infront of them
Chainlink Labs scammed you and you defend them on anonymous message boards
Theyre not gonna share that revenue with you bro

62484403
did u guys see this lmao >>62484143

62487489
>>62484352
>Theyre not gonna share that revenue with you
Chainlink revenue is shared with Link holders because more demand for Chainlink products directly translates to more demand for the Link token.
Because CL nodes must be paid in Link for literally everything they do, and there isn't a single Chainlink product, service, or feature that does not involve CL nodes.

If you wanna talk about revenue issues, the absolute worst mismatch I can think of in the crypto space is happening at Ripple.
They actively dump XRP and use that money to buy companies that don't use XRP at all but that boost revenue for Ripple shareholders.

62487541
What I like about these threads is how little people understand Link and what it does. It’s like watching retarded kids play football.

62487576
>>62487541
Chainlink makes Sergey rich and happy. Baggies get to defend him on 4chan.

62487620
>>62479415
>firsties
subhuman spotted kek. no white man ever uttered the word 'firsties'/'firstie'
you're the 36yo third worlder that shits himself because irritated colon and a cocaine addiction, aren't you? istg you vermin are so easy to spot

62487642
>>62487620
I don't even care about the whole fuddie-linkie war because its cringe but fudders turned out to be all european (apart from Adem who's turkish)

Linkies are mostly brown favela monkeys or literal indians that missed the bitcoin train. Check any Link conference

62487665
>>62487642
>check any LINK conference
>only posts the 2 or 3 community shitter events that were held outside of the West 5 years ago
>ignores literally all other events
You are persistent, I'll give you that. But what a strange hobby.

62487669
>>62487665
you got pissed because he called you a thirdie because you most likely are one

62487721
>>62487669
I'm not even the same guy, also I'm a Dutchfag

62487723
>>62487721
a dutch person wouldn't get pissed if he got called a turdie for shilling a dead altcoin on 4chan, Kumar

62487808
>>62487723
I'm not sure which conversation you are following, I'm calling you out for recycling the same 2-3 events not the other guy

62487821
>>62487808
you got PISSED as fuck because an anon said that altcoins are for shitskins (trvke), you aren't fooling anyone. How's the weather in Delhi?

62487877
>>62487821
>pls don't notice I've been spamming pics of the same 5 year old events ad nauseum here
>you're actually a thirdie who is mad at the other guy (totally not me hehe) for calling you a thirdie
You fudfags are so boring

62487885
>>62487877
okay sirs, I will not bad mouth the altcoins and chainlink. Keep the prayers र्गेई नाज़ारोव की तारीफ़ करें

62490703
Chainlink is like, electricity

62490746
>>62476675

62490762
>>62481749

None of you shills hold the token, you are all on the payroll.

62492634

OP

>Curious what everyone's plan is.

Early replies (first 3)

62475243
waiting for $10k and I will buy 3 btc

62475257
>>62475235
YOUR MOTHER

62475258
>>62475235
I'm DCAing bitcoin at the second half of the midterm year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62480040
>>62479203

62480706
>>62477951
You are him.

62480931
im buying its the cheapest it will ever get!

62481737
>>62480931

62481842
buy now

62483293
>>62481737

62483295
Look for Robinhood chainnnnnnnnnn

62483495
>>62476425
You're broke.

62483652
>>62483495
broke and broken hearted

62483662
>>62475235
Tomorrow will be a nice day for buying, he will dump it just like the last time

62484050
>>62483662
Short-term moves don't bother me much anymore. I'll keep accumulating and natively staking my BTC.

62484717
>>62484050
Do you have your BTC on a TBV? or how are you staking it natively?

62486316
>>62483293
Wait for 60k

62486462
>>62475258
been DCA at $100/week for a while. Total spent is $17,400 and total value is $17,888.

62486528
>>62483293
DCAing into Bitcoin in the second half of a midterm year usually works out pretty well. Remember, just knowing we're in a bear market isn't enough, you have to take action. You aren't going to time the exact bottom, but slowly accumulating when prices are low is how you navigate bear markets.

62486684
>>62484717
TBVs are still in the public testnet phase on Aave V4. Hopefully we'll see a launch later this year.

62487150
>>62486684
It's a boring market

62487536
>>62487150
How do you know? It's still in testnet. They're literally testing the use cases. Plenty of potential if it works.

62488078
>>62486528
You're making good money.

62488091
>>62486462
Time to take profit

62488156
>>62475235
at 40k yeah

62488664
>>62487536
We'll see. More ways to use native BTC are what will matter in the long run.

62488668
>>62488156
not possible

62489527
>>62483295
New hype chain lol

62489716
>>62475235
ahem

62490257
>>62488664
Babylon's probably the best place to keep up with BTCFi right now.

62490309
>>62488156
not 39k? Lol

62491802
Lfg

62491816
Anything around the 200 week moving average is a bargain.

I'm DCAing heavily at these levels. I want to get rid of all my cash by October.

62492766
>>62490257
Hard not to when they're working with partners like a16z crypto, Utila, and Aegis. Each one brings something different to the ecosystem.

item.title: It's Over: Zcash (ZEC) has defeated Monero (XMR) — 65 replies — 16.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's Over: Zcash (ZEC) has defeated Monero (XMR)

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-07-17 08:55 UTC

OP

I give up. It's over. With the news that zcash will eventually eliminate the transparent pool (see link below) it has utterly defeated the last argument that Monero ever had against it.

The argument against optional privacy was a pretty good one. Privacy shouldn't be something you have to opt into. Now zcash has patched up even that argument.

So what do you have? You have an extremely well funded coin with mandatory privacy, on all exchanges, with all the marketing and engineering budget to expand forever. Jews in support. Institutions coming. MSTR holding companies. Price skyrocketing. KOLs. Lobbyists. And the ONLY arguments against it are spurious and hard to prove. "Muh it has a backdoor" (can't be demonstrated) "Muh jews" (nobody cares) "Muh VCs" (nobody cares).

In comparison Monero is entirely self funded. Barely enough budget to keep its ~10 devs employed. No marketing at all. The only "success" it has is being used for acquiring fucking drugs on a couple of shady marketplaces nobody has ever heard of.

I loved what Monero stood for and I thought that investing in privacy would be a life changing financial decision. What actually happened was Jews stole your momentum, used it for themselves, adopted ALL of your tech and design decisions, ALL of your memes, and enriched and empowered themselves. Meanwhile you pretend your bazaar or drug market makes you superior. You, the loser goy, are destined to this life of serfdom and hardship. I thought I was amongst people who understood the reality of power. Apparently not.

https://x.com/Zcash/status/2077150354505118014

>$581 ZEC vs $332 XMR and climbing

Early replies (first 3)

62484203
>>62484065
Not buying your IDF tranny Mossad nigger coin

62484235
>>62484203
It doesn’t matter whether you and your 50 agorist friends buy anything. You’re completely market-irrelevant.

62484241
>>62484065
>"Muh jews" (nobody cares)
I do, and I'm not gonna use zcash. I'm just not, ok?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62486728
Mullvad doesn't accept your shitcoin

62486908
>>62486602
>growing global agorist sector.
I can’t believe you typed this unironically.

>compliant privacy without a clunky blockchain
All assets are moving on chain retard. It helps the institution not the customer.

>>62486039
You’re a giant faggot who doesn’t belong on the internet. Go visit a bathhouse and get raped.

>>62486719
Basically everyone who has invested in the last 5 years has made a ton of money more than Monero

62486910
>>62486728
Who literally gives the slightest shit also they will in the future

62486950
>>62486910
>>62486908
This feels like a false flag by a zcasher for bumps

62486968
>>62486950
False flags are a utility
Zcash is an investment (for making a LOT of money)

62486982
>>62486968
>Zcash is an investment
Based

62487147
How do you make an anonymous tip on the internet?
Paypal? Absolutely not anonymous.
BTC? Neither. You don't want to be exposed to chain analysis.

62487908
>>62487147
Zcash

62487928
>>62484203
FPBP

kys and your juice coin OP. XMR still the king in this space

62487933
>>62487908

62487988
>>62484065
>it has utterly defeated the last argument that Monero ever had against it.
Except that ZEC's argument for the transparent pool was compliance which caused its overvalued price.
>You have an extremely well funded coin with mandatory privacy, on all exchanges, with all the marketing and engineering budget to expand forever.
Which can just as easily make privacy optional again, has been caught needing ads by crypto influencers on twitter and will easily bend over backwards so the jews who hate privacy are pleased.
>And the ONLY arguments against it are spurious and hard to prove.
No DNM uses it
>The only "success" it has is being used for acquiring fucking drugs on a couple of shady marketplaces nobody has ever heard of.
Don't forget our CP, fuddy
>I loved what Monero stood for and I thought that investing in privacy would be a life changing financial decision. What actually happened was Jews stole your momentum, used it for themselves, adopted ALL of your tech and design decisions, ALL of your memes, and enriched and empowered themselves. Meanwhile you pretend your bazaar or drug market makes you superior. You, the loser goy, are destined to this life of serfdom and hardship. I thought I was amongst people who understood the reality of power. Apparently not.
Here it is. This isn't about tech or institutions or whatever. It's about one thing: Your bags.

62488407
>>62487988
>Your bags.
The perfect goy response, pretending to be morally superior because you’re attracting to PRINCIPLES not POWER. Kek

Go ahead and be a slave. Enjoy your slave morality religion. Toil in the field and work all day long until your body breaks down meanwhile you tell yourself your masters aren’t as good and moral as you because they don’t “work for their money.”

You are the lowest of the low. Scum of the earth. Destined to be a slave so you invent some justification story to tell yourself you’re not actually just a goy in the service of Jews.

Yes, yes, no one should EVER care about their “bags” aka money aka POWER. No one should ever care about how much power they have to cause change in the world and literally influence important events and decisions. No. That would be immoral.

Sure, the immigration and the social erosion and the manipulated money and the downfall of your race and civilization is all created out of thin air by people WHO HAVE POWER AKA MONEY. But you don’t play that game!! You’re too good for it. Very highly esteemed.

Pick up your shovel, goy. You have another acre to dig up.

62488457
If exchanges don't delist Zcash that means it's not truly private. Won't risk ever using a zio coin

62488481
>>62488407
>I once put 100 USD in, I deserve to be rich

62488523
>>62486908
>I can’t believe you typed this unironically.
Not a rebuttal. You don't have one so you deflect.

>All assets are moving on chain retard.
No they're not, retard, that's a bullshit marketing narrative. Financial assets already work better inside centralized high-throughput databases operated by banks, brokers and the like. Blockchains add unnecessary costs/overhead and suffer from lower throughput, higher operational complexity, irreversible error risk, oracle dependence and duplicated recordkeeping because legal ownership still ultimately depends on courts/registrars and regulated intermediaries i.e. sentient human beings pushing paper. Outside of permissionless p2p payments, blockchains are a solution in search of a problem.

The mainstream white market thus simply has no need for blockchains in general and privacy coins in particular. Nigerian ZEC baggies desperately push the white market adoption meme because ZEC ia profoundly toxic brand in black and grey markets where Monero has already established itself as a virtual monopoly. Nigerian ZEC baggies desperately need to convince us that ZEC is going places and we're missing out because that's the only way they can secure exit liquidity for themselves before its too late.

Still not buying your bags. Enjoy the pump while Barry, Artie and the Twinz bleed you dry.

62488548
>>62488523
>Financial assets already work better
For who? You? That’s not how institutions work. Blockchains - which you incorrectly assume will be public in this instance - are better for institutions and central banks in many ways.

>unnecessary costs/overhead and suffer from lower throughput, higher operational complexity, irreversible error risk, oracle dependence and duplicated recordkeeping
None of this will be a problem on JPMorganChain transacting with JPMUSD with one node (JPMorgan).

Your assets will be on a consortium corporate chain with proof of authority consensus. The benefit to the institution is immense, but we can leave it at the granular individualized settings you can apply to money when it literally becomes software.

62488719
I’m so happy my coin is run by sophisticated businessmen and Jews with long track records of venture capital, marketing and developing real companies. They know how to make money, connections, and partnerships that actually grow and thrive. It’s so easy to project the growth of zec given that it is held and promoted by these types of people. You’ll see zec everywhere. First on marketing. Twitter now, Times Square and Super Bowl ads later. The types of advertising people actually see. The the adoption. Institutions holding 1%+ in zec. Normies using existing exchange listings to constantly keep the price sustainably high. 100,000 is guaranteed - because zec is driven by pragmatism not ideology.

And what is Monero? A few people who lurk in dark corners who ideologically oppose marketing or growing the coin for its own sake. KEK. That has literally never worked in human history. But it’s okay because you can buy your magic mushrooms privately :) enjoy your amazing pump to $600 woohoooo!!!!!!!

62488724
>>62488548
>are better for institutions and central banks in many ways.
>None of this will be a problem on JPMorganChain
>Your assets will be on a consortium corporate chain with proof of authority consensus.
I love how you just claim things without any further reasoning. The wishful thinking is strong with this one.

Blockchain-based TXs are slow, clunky and highly susceptible to user error. We put up with these trade offs because its the only way to achieve truly permissionless transactions. Regulated institutions, however, obviously don't need their transactions to be permissionless so they simply don't have to accept any of these trade offs. Tech not needed.

62488738
>>62488724
Did you literally not understand anything that post said? No point in replying to you if you aren’t capable of understanding what I say

62488764
>>62488719
>And what is Monero?
Actually used.

Give it a rest, Mav, you're so predictable.

62488776
>>62488738
>Did you literally not understand anything that post said? No point in replying to you if you aren’t capable of understanding what I say
All you do is claim things without reasoning. Talk is cheap.

62488781
>>62488764
>Mav
Since when is Mav a zcasher kek

62488793
>>62488781
Mav is a simpleton who thinks he's being smart.

62489058
>>62484065
tranny coin
opinion disregarded
seethe more
fuck (((keynesians)))
monero is sound money, safe mode

62490416
Is ZCash better than Salvium or Zano tech wise ?

62491975
>>62484065
Is there any catalyst waiting for that can make it do 1000 or any level that will be a good dip? I just have no idea what to put my money on when I make it at seriously Walmart.

62492116
So the Zec trannies entire argument boils down to Zcash being a privacy memecoin (this is a good thing now) with CEX liquidity and benevolent e-celebs with no ulterior motive shill this memecoin all da wey to the supahbowl.

Lotta words to describe a pump and dump desu. Until I see an actual bad actor with actual skin in the game i.e. DNM/DPRK use Zcash in a significant manner to avoid literal torture and death in Guantanamo it will forever stay as a shitcoin in my eyes. Simple as!

62492550
>>62484542
>>62484621
>serious question...
the motivation for closing transparency is shielding the inflated supply, right?

62492557
>>62485979
yeah, it's go time. i remember how crestfallen i was when it was removed all those years ago.

62492586
>>62486719
>Nigerians
watch that space. despite, being an early adopter of cellphone e-cash with a history of currency crises, nigeria was an incubator for CBDC with authoritarian will to back it up. the early assumption was the third world would retain it's vestigial patchwork of release valves in response to authoritarian overreach be that seems to have become a dubious proposition.

item.title: Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late. — 163 replies — 14.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Open thread • Replies: 163 • Updated: 2026-07-17 01:51 UTC

OP

Sell SPCX you fools. Sell until it's too late.

Early replies (first 3)

62466535
They should just make corporate espionage legal in western countries at this point. The only people who are winning from the current laws are the Chinese

62466548
>>62466527
>state media says
lol

62466549
>>62466546
Show me one innovation from Japan.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62482168
>>62466549
Newhalf porn(?)

62482212
>>62482165
I think you're overselling its ai division. spacex is using models for materials research, starship design, research purposes exclusively. spacex will never make money directly from ai, and grok is not property of the company.

62484783
>>62466527
Below IPO price today ($132.70). I think $70 entry is interesting.

62485150
>>62466527
I kneel.

62485163
>>62485150
135 is the floor

62485166
I'm going to laugh so hard when Elon gets liquidated.

62485353
>>62466527
thanks just sold

62485386
>>62480129
even /sp/ has em jannies killed this board this thread is 5 days old

62485402
>>62466548
the chinese are pros at reverse engineering western tech. im sure they figured it out from all the footage spacex offers of their launches for free plus any spies they have in the company.

62485724
>>62485163
Sure

62485794
Basically I'm just not gonna sell. I know.. UGH I know.. IM SORRY! It's just that I'm not selling is all.

62485982
>>62467819
China produces a fuck ton of terrestrial based communication already to the point that it became a western security issue.
They are moving into space with the same tech they are certainly not lacking in capability.

62486000
>>62467844
There is an infinite amount of real, accurate data in the real world through cameras, microphones and various other sensors, alongside human communication and interaction.

AI doesn't need to run via text alone.

62486015
>>62485982
How many Chinese 5G towers in your country?

62486340
>>62468867
Bbc, german tvs, etc

62486345
>>62466527
They hodled

62486410
>>62466549
Nintendo

62486555
>>62486340
>Being a eurocuck
Sorry, I can't relate.

62486873
What about other space stocks, I'm pretty deep on rocket labs and lunr

62487093
>>62485402
the chinese version is essentially a giant net that it lands in, they don't catch the rocket like spacex does

62487201
Why does it matter that SPCX's float is only 4%? Explain like I'm a retard.

62487937
>>62486873
RKLB is smart in the way they position themselves around SPCX. They integrate vertically, have good connections to gov and biz and deliver launches. I keep them as secondary launch provider for redundancy reasons. And I hold.
LUNR is hit or miss (their fancy missions either succeed or fail) and I see neither the vision nor the ability or capacity to become more than that. Would I send payload with them? Only for the experience, not if it's something essential or expensive. I'll likely sell them at one point.

62487989
>>62466527
Most implessive ching chong ping ling don din baow

62488352
>>62487937
So are you still holding lunr?
It's been bleeding for a month.
Would you hold any other space stocks?

Other than silver miners idk where to store my cash

62488426
>>62487989
Unironically

62488803
>>62488352
>still holding lunr?
Because I bought it low (still in the green for me) and not too much of it.
>any other space stocks
SPCX for the long game, averaging in over the next years, I'm a customer since years and trust them a lot.
RKLB because they're solid, also for the long game. Unsure whether I'd buy today, but I'd be a customer w/o question.
NASDAQ:FLY has potential. They do good work, have a good track record. But they're niche and I'm not sure whether they can expand or even hold their position.
I bought LSE:SSIT (an investment trust that can invest in private equity) 3 years ago, but the management is woke (I met them) and they're opportunistic, not big game. Some like it, I don't.
That said, I'm more interested in private equity and cannot really give stock advice, my focus and expertise is also too narrow.

62489130
>>>>>impying SpaceX is a space company
Thanks for the AI hype money pit money, suckers.
@Grok how do I kill myself?
>>62487201
shitcoin with 96% of the coins already owned by the devs and a cabal of early investors that will dump on your ass soon

62489163
>>62467782
You save on weight like this and it's more consistent. There's a reason the early Starship renders had those big ass legs that were quietly swapped for light chopsticks later.

62491143
>>62466527
>Sell until it's too late.
brown hands typed this post

62491373
>>62466549
Blue LEDs
